---
title: "MP011: Principles System Architecture"
subtitle: "Three-part documentation structure and chapter numbering rules"
chapter: "CH00"
category: "MP"
number: "011"
date-created: "2025-04-01"
date-modified: "2026-01-23"
author: "Claude"
type: "meta-principle"
derives_from:
  - "MP001: Axiomatization System"
  - "MP034: Archive Immutability"
influences:
  - "MP002: Primitive Terms and Definitions"
  - "MP003: Structural Blueprint"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---


# Core Concept {#core-concept}

The 00_principles documentation system uses a stable three-part structure under `docs/` for all official content.

:::{.callout-important}
## Primary Documentation System
- `docs/` is the single source of truth.
- **Part 1: Principles** (MP, P, R)
- **Part 2: Implementations** (how to apply principles)
- **Part 3: Domain Knowledge** (domain-specific applications)
:::

# Chapter Range & Sequence Rules {#chapter-ranges}

This document defines the canonical chapter numbering rules used across all parts.

1. **No overlap across parts**: chapter numbers are globally unique.
2. **Sequential within each part**: no gaps; new chapters take the next available number.
3. **Ranges**:
   - **Part 1**: CH00-CH08 (Principles)
   - **Part 2**: starts at CH09 and increments sequentially
   - **Part 3**: starts after the last Part 2 chapter and increments sequentially
4. **Migration note**: legacy overlaps must be migrated to conform to this standard before adding new chapters.

# High-Level Structure {#structure}

```
00_principles/
`-- docs/
    |-- en/
    |   |-- part1_principles/
    |   |-- part2_implementations/
    |   `-- part3_domain_knowledge/
    `-- zh/ (mirror of en/)
```

# Scope Ownership {#scope-ownership}

- **Part 1 chapter scopes and classification** -> MP143
- **Part 3 domain mapping, prefixes, and inventories** -> SO_P019
- **Part 2 implementation details** -> `docs/en/part2_implementations/`

# Language Organization {#language-organization}

English and Chinese structures must mirror at the part/chapter level. When in doubt, update English first and then mirror in Chinese.
